message.content:
| Aspect                    | LLC                                               | S-Corp                                          | C-Corp                                         |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| **Taxation**              | | | |
| Income pass-through      | Yes, distributions are taxable to owners.        | Yes, owners pay individual income taxes on dividends and salary. | No, corporate income taxed at corporate level, then dividends taxed as personal income. |
| Pass-through taxation    | Yes, provisional tax payments may be required.  | Yes, with potential for self-employment tax.    | No, corporate income taxed at corporate level. |
| **Ownership**             | | | |
| Number of owners         | Unlimited (practical limit about 35-50).         | Up to 100 natural persons.                      | Unlimited, but with certain shareholding restrictions. |
| Ownership transfer       | Generally easy via membership interest transfer. | Generally cumbersome, requires shareholder agreement. | Complex, subject to SEC registration and filing. |
| **Paperwork**             | | | |
| Filing requirements      | Annual report, state tax registration.            | Articles of incorporation, bylaws, shareholder agreement. | Significant annual filings, SEC registration, stock options. |
| Compliance             | Generally less; minimal compliance.              | More strict; annual reporting, shareholder meetings, audit requirements. | Rigorous compliance; annual reports, SEC filings, board meetings, disclosure statements. |
| **Pros**                 | | | |
| Flexibility              | Highly flexible (pass-through, tax status).     | Fixed (corporate structure, rules for sharing profits and losses). | Established, reputable; easier to raise capital. |
| Liability protection     | Yes, similar to a corporation.                  | Yes, but stricter rules.                        | Yes, but corporate structure may affect liability. |
| **Cons**                 | | | |
| Self-employment tax      | No, but may owe estimated taxes.                 | Potentially high; includes self-employment tax.  | No, but corporate structure and stock options can complicate ownership and financing. |
| Initial setup cost       | Lower startup cost.                             | Higher, with lawyer and accountant fees.       | Highest, significant legal and audit costs. |
| Potential double taxation | Not provisional.                                 | Potential for double taxation (in dividends).   | Double taxation (corporate and personal income). |
| Complexity               | Relatively simple.                              | More complex.                                   | Highly complex. |

This table provides a concise comparison of the key aspects of LLCs, S-corps, and C-corps for a U.S. small business owner, highlighting the main differences in taxation, ownership, paperwork, pros, and cons.
